major_achievements.achievement: Served as CEO of SGX, leading Singapore's securities and derivatives exchange.
Served as Chairman of UOB, one of Singapore's Big Three banks.
Served as Executive Director and President of Temasek Holdings, Singapore's sovereign wealth fund.
Co-founded PrimePartners Group, a significant financial services group in Asia.
Served as President of NCSS, championing social services in Singapore.

biography_summary: Hsieh Fu Hua is a distinguished Singaporean financial services executive and industry veteran with over 35 years of experience in merchant banking and capital markets. A graduate of NUS Business School, he began his career in 1974 at Morgan Grenfell Asia Holdings, eventually rising to head the organisation. He subsequently served as Group Managing Director of BNP Prime Peregrine Group in Hong Kong, CEO of Singapore Exchange (2003-2009), and Executive Director and President of Temasek Holdings (2010-2011). He co-founded PrimePartners Group and has served as Chairman of UOB (2012-2017), Chairman of GXS Bank (a Grab-Singtel digital banking joint venture), Chairman of ACR Capital Holdings, and Chairman of NUS Board of Trustees. He is currently a Director on the GIC Board and active in non-profit and social service sectors.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with data from GIC, PrimePartners, MarketsWiki, and TheOfficialBoard. Full career spanning Morgan Grenfell, BNP Prime Peregrine, SGX, Temasek, UOB, and GIC added. Education and biography added.
